NousResearch/hermes-agent.summary: Hermes Agent is an extensible AI assistant framework designed for persistent, cross-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to autonomously create and refine skills from experience while building long-term user models through Honcho dialectic modeling. The system supports 14 messaging platforms and six execution backends, including Docker and SSH, allowing it to run on everything from local terminals to serverless cloud infrastructure. Users can swap between over 200 LLM providers without code changes, utilizing built-in tools for scheduling, parallel subagent delegation, and research trajectory generation.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement from task experience
Supports 14 platforms including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp
Six execution backends including Docker, SSH, and serverless Modal

NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling.summary: This repository provides the official implementation and examples for function calling and structured JSON output using Hermes 2 Pro models. It utilizes a recursive inference loop and ChatML prompting to enable the model to execute tools, process external data, and return validated responses. Users can define custom tools via Python functions or specify data structures using Pydantic models for schema-adherent extraction. The project includes pre-configured financial tools using the yfinance library to demonstrate real-world utility in retrieving stock market data.
highlights: Enables recursive function calling and multi-turn tool execution loops
Supports structured data extraction via Pydantic-defined JSON mode
Uses ChatML format for OpenAI-compatible system and tool messaging

NousResearch/atropos.summary: Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning (RL) with large language models. It functions by setting up environments as services that interact with a trajectory API, allowing trainers to pull batches of data for model optimization. The system supports a wide range of interactive settings, including code execution, multi-turn tool calling, and multimodal tasks. By standardizing how models collect and evaluate trajectories, it enables specialized training for complex reasoning and functional tasks.
highlights: Microservice architecture for scalable asynchronous reinforcement learning environments
Supports dataset, online game, and multi-turn tool calling environments
Includes specialized servers for code execution and multimodal evaluation

NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution.summary: Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.
highlights: Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter.summary: The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that integrates Hermes Agent into the Paperclip company management system as a managed employee. It functions by wrapping the Hermes CLI, parsing raw terminal output into structured transcript entries and GFM markdown for clean rendering in the Paperclip UI. The adapter enables agents to respond to task assignments or issue comments while maintaining persistent memory and session continuity across heartbeats. It also synchronizes local and managed skills, allowing Hermes to utilize its full suite of 30+ native tools and MCP servers within a corporate organizational structure.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agent to Paperclip as a managed autonomous employee
Parses raw CLI output into structured tool cards and markdown
Supports 8 inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

NousResearch/autonovel.summary: Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.
highlights: Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

outsourc-e/hermes-workspace.summary: Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to orchestrate Hermes Agents through a unified graphical interface. It connects to the Hermes Agent gateway to provide real-time agent interaction, memory browsing, and skill management. The platform supports Swarm Mode for multi-agent coordination, featuring a Kanban task board and persistent tmux-backed workers for autonomous task execution.
highlights: Unified interface for chat, terminal, memory, and skills
Swarm Mode for multi-agent orchestration and role-based dispatch
Mobile-first PWA support with real-time SSE streaming

fathah/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native companion application that provides a graphical interface for installing, configuring, and interacting with Hermes Agent. It automates the setup process by managing dependencies like Python and Git, allowing users to run the agent locally or connect to a remote API server. The application features a streaming chat UI with support for 22 slash commands, session management via SQLite, and a persona editor for modifying the agent's personality. It integrates with 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, including web browsing, code execution, and task planning.
highlights: Guided installer for local or remote Hermes Agent backends
Supports 16 messaging gateways including Discord, Slack, and Telegram
Integrated management for memory systems, toolsets, and scheduled tasks

sanchomuzax/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a monitoring and configuration dashboard designed to provide visibility into Hermes Agent operations. It functions by using a FastAPI backend to read the agent's existing SQLite database and YAML files via a WebSocket-based polling bridge, requiring no modifications to the core agent code. Users can track real-time activity, inspect conversation histories, and manage scheduled cron jobs through a responsive interface. The tool also provides read-only access to system configurations and skill source code for easier oversight.
highlights: Real-time dashboard for session activity and cost tracking
Full conversation history inspection with FTS5 search
Read-only configuration and skill source code viewing

Euraika-Labs/pan-ui.summary: Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to provide a comprehensive management interface for the Hermes Agent. It functions as a Next.js server that bridges the browser to the Hermes runtime, automatically launching the Hermes gateway if it is not already active. Users can manage isolated profile workspaces, browse and install skills from skills.sh, and monitor real-time agent operations through tool timelines and audit trails. The interface also provides dedicated controls for Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, memory inspection, and session resumption across platforms.
highlights: Chat-first workspace with SSE streaming and tool execution timelines
Integrated marketplace for discovering and installing skills and MCP servers
Isolated profile management for separate sessions, memory, and API keys

mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills.summary: Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source library of 754 structured security skills designed to provide AI agents with senior-level practitioner workflows. The project utilizes the agentskills.io standard, providing YAML frontmatter for discovery and structured Markdown for step-by-step execution across 26 security domains. Each skill is uniquely mapped to five industry frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with platforms such as Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot to assist in threat hunting, forensics, and incident response.
highlights: 754 structured skills across 26 specialized security domains
Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks
Compatible with Hermes Agent and agentskills.io open standard

conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing.summary: Avoid-ai-writing is a portable writing skill designed to audit and rewrite text to eliminate detectable AI patterns and "AI-isms." It utilizes a structured two-pass process that first identifies issues like significance inflation and copula avoidance, then performs a second audit to catch any lingering tells. The tool relies on a 109-entry word replacement table and 36 specific pattern categories to ensure edits are grounded in plain language rather than vague prompts. It is compatible with Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, offering both a full rewrite mode and a detection-only mode for auditing content.
highlights: Identifies 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells
Uses a three-tier, 109-entry word replacement table for objective editing
Performs a two-pass audit to catch recycled transitions and inflation

wondelai/skills.summary: Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.
highlights: Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills.summary: pydantic-ai-skills is a framework that implements the Agent Skills open specification to provide modular, specialized capabilities for Pydantic AI agents. It uses a tool-calling approach to enable progressive disclosure, allowing agents to discover and load detailed instructions or scripts only when relevant to a task. The package supports skills defined as filesystem directories or created programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses. This architecture reduces token consumption by preventing the injection of unnecessary context into the initial system prompt.
highlights: Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

Agents365-ai/drawio-skill.summary: The drawio-skill project is a multi-platform agent tool that converts natural language descriptions into professional .drawio diagrams. It utilizes the native draw.io desktop CLI to generate XML files and export them to formats like PNG, SVG, and PDF with grid-aligned layouts. The tool features six specialized presets for architecture, machine learning, and UML diagrams, including unique capabilities like tensor shape annotations and animated connectors. It supports an iterative design loop where the agent can self-check, auto-fix, and refine diagrams based on user feedback across various AI coding platforms.
highlights: Generates .drawio XML and exports to PNG, SVG, or PDF
Six diagram presets including ML models with tensor shape annotations
Self-correcting iterative loop with auto-fix for layout and style issues

smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills.summary: Chainlink Agent Skills is a collection of standardized tools that enable AI agents to interact with Chainlink's oracle network and smart contract infrastructure. The project implements the Agent Skills specification, allowing developers to add specific capabilities to their agents via a CLI-based installation process. These skills facilitate complex tasks such as cross-chain transfers via CCIP, data feed integration, and real-time data stream processing. The repository also includes local evaluation frameworks for A/B testing skill performance and refining agent responses.
highlights: Standardized skills for CCIP, Data Feeds, and Data Streams integration
CLI-based installation compatible with the open skills ecosystem
Local A/B testing framework for evaluating and improving agent responses

tlehman/litprog-skill.summary: The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.
highlights: Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

esaradev/icarus-plugin.summary: Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.
highlights: Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

chigwell/skilldock.io.summary: SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to search, install, and version-control modular agent capabilities. The system supports recursive dependency resolution, local skill verification, and a dual-rail commerce flow for buying and selling skills using Stripe or TON. Developers can publish private or public skills by uploading packaged ZIP archives with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.
highlights: OpenAPI-driven Python SDK and CLI for skill discovery and management
Automated dependency resolution and local installation of agent skills
Integrated commerce system supporting Stripe and TON blockchain payments

black-forest-labs/skills.summary: This project provides official image generation skills for FLUX models, offering standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns following the agentskills.io specification. It functions as a technical resource for developers to implement text-to-image and image-to-image workflows through structured JSON prompting, hex color specification, and multi-reference editing. The repository includes a model selection guide for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 variants and can be integrated directly into Claude Code as a plugin. Users can access documented API patterns for asynchronous polling, webhook verification, and rate limit management.
highlights: Standardized prompting guidelines for FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 model families
API integration patterns for polling, webhooks, and error handling
Support for JSON structured prompting and precise hex color specification

Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory.summary: Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.
highlights: Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

ReinaMacCredy/maestro.summary: Maestro is a local-first conductor for multi-agent software engineering that manages shared state across separate agent sessions. It uses a CLI to persist missions, features, and memory within a local .maestro directory, removing the need for external databases or background daemons. The tool enables a plan-approve-execute workflow where human operators coordinate agents through structured handoffs and markdown briefs. This approach ensures that project context and learning remain disciplined and inspectable across different model vendors and terminal tools.
highlights: Persists shared agent state locally in .maestro directory
Orchestrates work through missions, milestones, and validation assertions
Generates markdown briefs for fresh Codex or Claude handoffs

tiann/execplan-skill.summary: ExecPlan is an Agent Skill designed to help AI coding agents autonomously manage complex, long-running implementation tasks. It utilizes a structured execution plan that allows users to review and approve the approach before implementation starts. The system tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback procedures for safe recovery. It is compatible with tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Goose.
highlights: Autonomous execution for long-running tasks
Real-time tracking of progress and decisions
Built-in rollback procedures for safe recovery

cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill.summary: Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client and daemon designed to optimize token consumption through a three-layer lazy loading architecture. It implements the AgentSkills.io pattern by progressively disclosing server metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when requested by the agent. This approach reduces context window overhead by up to 86% compared to loading full tool definitions upfront. The project includes a socket-based daemon for persistent server management and a CLI for manual or automated tool execution.
highlights: Three-layer progressive disclosure reduces token usage by loading tools on-demand
Socket-based daemon maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers
Hot-reload support allows configuration updates without restarting the daemon

armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge.summary: Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discourse into verified instruction files for AI agents. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) analysis to extract precise function signatures and types, ensuring agents use libraries correctly without hallucinating API calls. Every generated instruction includes a provenance map linking back to specific file lines at pinned commits or documentation URLs. The tool supports lifecycle management for versioned skills and exports files compliant with the agentskills.io specification for use in environments like Cursor, Claude, and Copilot.
highlights: Generates verified AI agent instructions from code and documentation
Traces every instruction to specific file lines and pinned commits
Supports version-pinned skill storage to prevent instruction rot

amanning3390/hermeshub.summary: HermesHub is a curated skills registry for Hermes Agent that provides a secure way to browse, install, and share AI agent capabilities. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. The platform includes a creator marketplace supporting x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skills, as well as an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scoring.
highlights: Automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules
Creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe-based payment protocols
Agent-to-agent feedback system for reliability and trust scores

PederHP/skillsdotnet.summary: SkillsDotNet is a C# implementation of the agentskills.io specification that enables Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for agentic workflows. It uses a "context-as-progressive-disclosure" approach where agents initially load small frontmatter summaries and only fetch full SKILL.md content on demand. The library provides a standardized skill:// URI convention for distributing resources and includes a SkillCatalog for managing skills across multiple MCP servers. It features built-in support for discovering skills from common agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.
highlights: Implements agentskills.io specification for C# and .NET environments
Provides MCP resource templates for on-demand skill and file loading
Integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for automated tool-based skill discovery

mem0ai/mem0.summary: Mem0 is a persistent memory layer designed to provide AI agents with long-term context and personalization across different sessions. It utilizes a single-pass extraction algorithm that accumulates facts, links entities, and employs multi-signal retrieval combining semantic, keyword, and entity matching. This approach allows agents to recall user preferences and past interactions without the need for complex agentic loops or manual state management. The system is available as a self-hosted Apache-2.0 library or a managed cloud service, supporting diverse use cases from customer support to healthcare.
highlights: Multi-signal retrieval fusing semantic, BM25 keyword, and entity matching.
Single-pass memory extraction without overwriting or deleting existing data.
Supports User, Session, and Agent-level memory for adaptive personalization.

vectorize-io/hindsight.summary: Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to help AI agents learn from experiences rather than just storing conversation history. It utilizes a retain, recall, and reflect workflow to build mental models that improve performance on long-term memory tasks. The system can be integrated via a two-line LLM wrapper or a direct API to automate memory storage and retrieval. It supports per-user memory isolation through metadata filtering and is compatible with major providers like OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
highlights: Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term learning
Provides a two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management
Supports PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database for enterprise storage

greyhaven-ai/autocontext.summary: Autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness that enables AI agents to succeed on complex tasks through iterative evaluation and refinement. It operates by running a multi-role loop—including competitors, analysts, and curators—that tests strategies against real-world scenarios and retains successful outcomes. The system produces structured traces, playbooks, and datasets that allow future agent iterations to inherit accumulated knowledge. It integrates with various providers like Anthropic and OpenAI, and offers specialized support for Hermes Agent and Claude Code via MCP.
highlights: Iterative self-improvement loop using five specialized agent roles
Generates structured playbooks and traces for knowledge persistence
Native integration for Hermes Agent and MCP-compatible clients

elkimek/honcho-self-hosted.summary: This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.
highlights: Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

yoloshii/ClawMem.summary: ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine designed to provide persistent knowledge across different AI agent runtimes. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval architecture that combines vector search, BM25, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant project history and decisions. The system automatically captures milestones and preferences from session transcripts using a local observer model, storing everything in a shared SQLite vault. It integrates directly with Hermes agents, Claude Code, and OpenClaw to ensure context remains consistent as users switch between different tools.
highlights: Hybrid retrieval using vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal
Local-only operation with no cloud dependencies or API keys required
Cross-runtime memory sharing between Hermes, OpenClaw, and Claude Code

plur-ai/plur.summary: PLUR is a local-first persistent memory layer designed to synchronize AI agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk, using a hybrid retrieval system of BM25 and local embeddings to inject relevant context into agent prompts without cloud dependencies. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where memories strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to maintain context quality. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools like Claude Code and Cursor, as well as Hermes Agent via a dedicated plugin.
highlights: Stores persistent agent memory as local YAML engrams
Hybrid BM25 and embedding retrieval with zero API costs
Automatic memory injection for Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes

amanning3390/flowstate-qmd.summary: FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.
highlights: Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus.summary: Hermes Web Search Plus is a multi-provider search plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables intelligent information retrieval from ten different search engines. The system uses an auto-routing engine to select the most appropriate provider, such as Exa for semantic discovery or Serper for news, based on the specific intent of the user's query. It features adaptive fallback logic to skip providers on cooldown and includes a dedicated tool for extracting clean markdown content from URLs. Users can further refine results through time-range filtering, domain restrictions, and specialized deep-research modes for complex synthesis.
highlights: Auto-routes queries across 10 providers including Brave, Exa, and Tavily
Supports deep research and cross-document analysis via Exa integration
Includes URL extraction tool for converting web content to markdown

42-evey/hermes-plugins.summary: Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.
highlights: Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin.summary: The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.
highlights: Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin.summary: The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.
highlights: Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

nativ3ai/hermes-payguard.summary: Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.
highlights: Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare.summary: This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.
highlights: Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

builderz-labs/mission-control.summary: Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.
highlights: Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

swarmclawai/swarmclaw.summary: SwarmClaw is a self-hosted runtime designed for orchestrating and managing autonomous AI agent swarms. It functions as a central hub for multi-agent delegation, providing agents with persistent memory, runtime skills, and scheduled heartbeats. The platform integrates with over 20 model providers and CLI-based agents, including Hermes, OpenClaw, and Claude Code. Users can visualize agent hierarchies through an organizational chart and manage reviewed conversation-to-skill learning to expand agent capabilities over time.
highlights: Native support for MCP and 23 built-in model providers
Visualizes agent teams and delegation through an interactive org chart
Supports durable conversation history and reviewed skill learning

howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness.summary: Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system automates benchmark orchestration, archive analysis, and frontier tracking to identify high-performing harness candidates. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, providing structured mutation and search capabilities to refine agentic workflows.
highlights: Optimizes harness code through an automated outer-loop search process
Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluation via Hermes Agent
Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

runtimenoteslabs/gladiator.summary: Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.
highlights: Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent.summary: Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI to automate complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through a strict pipeline of 17 specialized agents covering research, planning, implementation, quality, documentation, and infrastructure. The system enforces mandatory quality gates and security audits while maintaining context flow across different LLM models. It supports diverse workflows including feature development, bug investigation, and performance optimization with built-in revision loops.
highlights: Coordinates 17 specialized agents using a master orchestrator architecture
Implements mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing
Automates workflows for research, implementation, security, and infrastructure tasks

supermodeltools/bigiron.summary: Big Iron is an experimental AI-native software development system that automates the full development lifecycle through a graph-gated workflow. It integrates Hermes Agent with the Supermodel Code Graph API to replace traditional file-loading context with structural intelligence, including call graphs and dependency maps. The system executes an eight-phase process—from planning to autonomous health checks—using specific skills and quality gates to ensure architectural alignment. While now deprecated in favor of the Supermodel CLI, it serves as a prototype for autonomous, graph-grounded code generation and refactoring.
highlights: Executes an autonomous eight-phase SDLC from planning to health checks
Uses Supermodel MCP to provide Hermes with structural code graphs
Enforces architectural quality through automated graph-diff phase gates

Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill.summary: The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.
highlights: Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

numtide/llm-agents.nix.summary: llm-agents.nix is a collection of Nix packages designed to simplify the deployment and management of AI coding agents and development tools. The project provides a centralized repository of Nix expressions that automatically update daily to ensure users have access to the latest versions of various CLI-based agents. It supports a wide range of tools including multi-agent orchestrators, terminal-based coding assistants, and unified API proxies for major LLM providers. By leveraging the Nix package manager, it enables reproducible environments for tools like Goose, Claude Code, and various community-driven agent frameworks.
highlights: Provides Nix packages for numerous AI coding agents and CLI tools
Automated daily updates ensure access to the latest software versions
Supports diverse tools including orchestrators, proxies, and terminal-based assistants

TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict network egress, filesystem access, and system calls. The environment supports persistent memory, self-improving skills, and multi-platform messaging gateways while maintaining a strict security posture. Users can toggle between predefined policy presets to balance agent autonomy with safety constraints.
highlights: Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking
Multi-layer security covering network egress, filesystem, and process isolation
Extensible library of 40+ built-in tools and self-improving skills

0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent.summary: nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.
highlights: Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent.summary: Portable Hermes Agent is a Windows-native desktop application that provides a user-friendly interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It operates through a Tkinter-based GUI or CLI, utilizing an embedded Python environment to connect with local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The agent features a library of 100 tools for tasks including music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation. Users can also build custom automation pipelines through a built-in workflow engine and a dynamic tool maker for wrapping REST APIs.
highlights: Zero-install Windows agent with 100 built-in tools across 20 toolsets
Local model support via LM Studio and portable AI generation servers
Dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation pipelines

xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker.summary: This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.
highlights: Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
Supports persistent state via mounted home directories
Allows building specific Hermes tags using build arguments

JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server.summary: This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.
highlights: Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes.summary: This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.
highlights: Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

raulvidis/hermes-android.summary: Hermes-android is a remote automation bridge that enables Hermes agents to control Android devices through a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset. The system establishes an outbound WebSocket connection from the phone to a server, bypassing the need for port forwarding or USB cables via a 6-character pairing code. It provides 36 specialized tools for UI interaction, including screen reading, gesture execution, and system-level actions like making calls or sending SMS. This integration allows agents to perform autonomous mobile tasks such as checking notifications, managing contacts, and navigating third-party applications.
highlights: Executes 36 tools for UI navigation, media control, and system actions
Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and coordinate-based gesture automation
Connects via WebSocket relay to work behind NAT and firewalls

teknium1/hermes-miniverse.summary: Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.
highlights: Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council.summary: Hermes-council is an MCP server designed to provide adversarial, multi-perspective decision-making for the hermes-agent ecosystem. It functions by orchestrating five distinct personas—Advocate, Skeptic, Oracle, Contrarian, and Arbiter—to debate complex questions through structured deliberation. The system produces calibrated verdicts with confidence scores and evidence links, and it can be used to generate DPO preference pairs for Reinforcement Learning training.
highlights: Five distinct intellectual personas for adversarial deliberation
Generates DPO preference pairs for RL training
Supports custom persona configuration via YAML

marlandoj/zouroboros-swarm-executors.summary: Zouroboros Swarm Executors is a bridge system that integrates local CLI-based AI tools into the Zo Computer swarm orchestration ecosystem. It functions by using bash bridge scripts to wrap tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, allowing them to receive tasks via standard input and return results via standard output. This approach enables local execution of complex tasks such as security audits and web research while maintaining a shared identity and memory system across all personas. The project includes a health-check system to validate environment configurations and a registry for adding custom CLI tools as swarm members.
highlights: Wraps CLI tools into AI personas with shared identity files
Provides bridge scripts for Hermes, Claude Code, Gemini, and Codex
Routes tasks locally instead of through remote API calls

gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle.summary: Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.
highlights: Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

junhoyeo/tokscale.summary: Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and associated costs across various AI coding agents. It works by scanning local data directories, databases, and session logs from supported clients to aggregate usage statistics into a native Rust TUI or a web-based dashboard. The tool provides detailed daily summaries, model-specific breakdowns, and a 3D contribution graph for visualizing activity. It supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and Cursor IDE.
highlights: Tracks token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents
Features a native Rust TUI and 3D visualization dashboard
Integrates with Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI

joeynyc/hermes-skins.summary: Hermes Skins is a collection of visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent that allows users to customize the terminal user interface. It uses YAML configuration files to modify elements such as banner colors, ASCII art, spinner verbs, and branding text without altering the agent's behavior. Users can apply these skins per session or permanently via a config file, with missing values automatically inheriting from a default theme.
highlights: Customizes colors, ASCII banners, and UI labels via YAML
Includes diverse themes like retro DOS, cyberpunk, and brand tributes
Supports partial skin definitions through default theme inheritance

AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC.summary: hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.
highlights: Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser.summary: Vessel is an open-source Chromium-based browser designed specifically for autonomous agent control and human supervision. It provides a persistent runtime with durable state, allowing external agent harnesses to drive workflows while humans audit and intervene through a visible UI. The browser features a built-in Model Context Protocol (MCP) interface, automated page schema inference, and session management to support long-running web tasks. It integrates with various AI providers and includes specialized tools like action undo and agent-meaningful bookmarks for structured navigation.
highlights: Persistent browser sessions with named state and cookie management
MCP-native control for external agent harnesses like Hermes Agent
Automated page schema inference for structured content and form extraction

Abruptive/Ankh.md.summary: Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.
highlights: Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

roli-lpci/lintlang.summary: lintlang is a static linter designed to identify structural and linguistic failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It utilizes regex and AST-based heuristics rather than LLM calls to detect issues like vague tool descriptions and missing termination conditions. This approach ensures deterministic, offline-capable validation that runs in approximately 2ms per file. By catching language-level bugs before runtime, it prevents common agent failures such as infinite loops and incorrect tool selection.
highlights: Deterministic structural analysis using regex and AST instead of LLMs
Detects seven specific failure modes including tool ambiguity and role confusion
Integrates into CI pipelines with zero API costs or network latency

0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes.summary: openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.
highlights: Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

42-evey/evey-setup.summary: Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.
highlights: Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

ucsandman/DashClaw.summary: DashClaw is a decision infrastructure designed to provide governance and risk assessment for AI agents interacting with external systems. It functions as a middleware layer that intercepts agent decisions, evaluates them against established policies, and records verifiable evidence of every action. The platform supports a wide range of frameworks including LangChain, CrewAI, and AutoGen, and offers specialized integration for Claude Code via MCP or automated hooks. This allows users to approve high-risk tool calls through external interfaces like Discord to prevent destructive autonomous actions.
highlights: Intercepts agent decisions to enforce guard policies
Provides verifiable evidence and decision replay records
Supports Claude Code via MCP and automated hooks

Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander.summary: Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and apply fixes across CPU, memory, disk, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.
highlights: Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically.
Integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time P0 alerts.

Christabel337/job-scout-agent.summary: Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.
highlights: Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

rodmarkun/anihermes.summary: AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.
highlights: Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-moni.summary: This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous infrastructure monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to automatically ingest AI research from arXiv and track trending repositories on GitHub. The system analyzes these signals to detect infrastructure risks, identify compute bottlenecks, and generate cost projection models. Output is delivered through structured Markdown reports and a terminal-style dashboard that summarizes ecosystem trends and operational risks.
highlights: Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem tracking.
Scheduled cron workflows for risk analysis and cost modeling.
Generates structured Markdown reports and ASCII infrastructure dashboards.

bigph00t/hermescraft.summary: HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.
highlights: Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

hxsteric/mercury.summary: Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.
highlights: Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

bryercowan/hermes-embodied.summary: Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.
highlights: Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent.summary: This repository is a curated directory of resources, skills, and integrations for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by Nous Research. It centralizes community-developed tools, including skill factories that automate workflow creation and specialized plugins for goal management and cost control. The collection provides a structured path for users to transition from basic installation to advanced multi-agent orchestration and autonomous task execution.
highlights: Categorizes community skills by maturity levels from experimental to production
Indexes official Nous Research projects like autonovel and self-evolution pipelines
Provides quickstart paths for GUIs, skill libraries, and deployment tools

alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book.summary: The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.
highlights: Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide.summary: The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive technical resource and toolkit designed to streamline the deployment and performance tuning of Hermes Agent v0.12.0. It provides a 23-part instructional series alongside runnable artifacts, including a one-command VPS bootstrap script and opinionated configuration templates for various production scenarios. The project enables users to integrate Hermes with over 18 platforms, manage remote sandboxes, and implement observability through Langfuse. It specifically addresses practical challenges such as cost-optimized model routing, security hardening against prompt injection, and automated skill curation.
highlights: One-command VPS bootstrap script for Debian and Ubuntu production environments
Thirteen installable skills and five pre-configured YAML templates for deployment
Supports 18+ platforms including Telegram, Discord, Slack, and enterprise tools

metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu.summary: This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation script for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2, Linux, or macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with Qwen3.5 GGUF models, utilizing CUDA or Metal acceleration for optimized performance. The guide covers the full stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. Users can deploy a production-ready agentic environment with local LLM support and OpenAI-compatible API endpoints.
highlights: Automated installation script for Hermes Agent, llama.cpp, and Camofox
Supports GPU acceleration via CUDA for WSL2 and Metal for macOS
Configures Qwen3.5 GGUF models for local inference and web browsing

mudrii/hermes-agent-docs.summary: Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform deployment beyond local environments. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create reusable skills, maintain long-term memory in SQLite, and perform full-text searches across past conversation sessions. The agent supports execution across 17 messaging platforms, CLI interfaces, and IDEs while offering flexible model provider integration through a pluggable transport layer.
highlights: Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
Unified messaging gateway supporting 17 platforms including Discord and Telegram
Extensible plugin architecture for custom tools, commands, and hooks

martymcenroe/HermesWiki.summary: HermesWiki is a dedicated documentation repository that serves as a central knowledge base for the Hermes autonomous AI email agent. It provides structured guidance on how to implement and manage the agent's email-based workflows. Users can access deployment patterns and practical recipes to optimize their specific agent configurations. This resource ensures that developers have a clear reference for integrating the AI agent into their existing communication systems.
highlights: Centralized documentation for the Hermes autonomous email agent
Provides practical deployment patterns for various environments
Contains specific recipes for configuring AI email workflows

nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel.summary: This project is a specialized fork of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL trust-boundary principles to defend against prompt-injection attacks during autonomous execution. It implements a security layer adapted from Google Research that monitors or enforces policy decisions when the agent interacts with untrusted contexts. Users can explicitly opt into these protections using specific CLI flags to block unauthorized sensitive actions without altering the core Hermes runtime. The agent retains its original capabilities, including self-improving skills, cross-platform messaging integration, and a built-in learning loop for persistent memory.
highlights: Implements CaMeL trust-boundary principles for prompt-injection defense
Offers monitor and enforce modes for sensitive tool authorization
Maintains full compatibility with upstream Hermes Agent features

kaminocorp/hermes-alpha.summary: Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.
highlights: Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation.summary: This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from the Hermes agent and converts them into high-quality training data via a specialized environment called RealWorldTaskEnv. The system automatically scores these trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery to create SFT-ready datasets or live RL training loops. By integrating with the Atropos framework, it allows the model to learn from practical tasks like coding, web research, and system administration.
highlights: Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from 30 diverse real-world task categories
Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning via GRPO training
Automated multi-dimensional reward scoring for task completion and efficiency

xaspx/hermes-control-interface.summary: Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to simplify the management of the Hermes AI agent stack. It utilizes a Node.js and Express backend with a Vanilla JS and Vite frontend to provide a browser-based administrative layer. The tool enables users to monitor system health, manage multiple agent profiles, and analyze token usage and costs. It also includes integrated utilities for file editing, terminal access, and cron job scheduling.
highlights: Multi-agent gateway management with real-time logs and profile configuration
Detailed token analytics tracking costs, models, and platform usage
Integrated browser-based terminal and secure file explorer

ksimback/hermes-ecosystem.summary: Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.
highlights: Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

dodo-reach/hermes-desktop.summary: Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a localized workspace for managing Hermes Agent hosts over SSH. It connects directly to remote environments to manage sessions, skills, and cron jobs without requiring a gateway API, local file mirroring, or remote daemon. The app features a multi-tabbed terminal and profile-aware views that surface usage trends and canonical file states directly from the host. This allows users to maintain a persistent, native interface for multi-agent workflows while keeping the remote server as the single source of truth.
highlights: Direct SSH connection with no remote helper service required
Profile-aware management of sessions, skills, and cron jobs
Native multi-tabbed terminal with live theme and color controls

clawshell/clawshell.summary: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.
highlights: Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

nesquena/hermes-webui.summary: Hermes WebUI is a lightweight browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent, providing a graphical alternative to the standard command-line environment. It utilizes a Python backend and vanilla JavaScript frontend to deliver a three-panel layout for managing chat sessions, workspace files, and agent settings without requiring complex build steps or frameworks. The interface maintains full parity with the CLI, allowing users to interact with the agent's persistent memory, scheduled jobs, and self-improving skills from any device. It integrates directly with existing Hermes configurations and supports secure remote access via SSH tunneling or Docker deployment.
highlights: Three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management
Full feature parity with the Hermes Agent command-line interface
Visual context ring for real-time monitoring of token usage

garrytan/gbrain.summary: GBrain is a persistent memory and knowledge graph system designed to prevent AI agents from losing context over time. It utilizes a self-wiring graph architecture that automatically extracts entity relationships and creates typed links without requiring LLM calls. The system combines hybrid search with backlink-boosted ranking to outperform standard vector-only RAG approaches. It integrates with Hermes Agent and OpenClaw via MCP, providing 29 specialized skills for tasks like meeting ingestion, code symbol analysis, and autonomous memory consolidation.
highlights: Self-wiring knowledge graph with automated entity and relationship extraction
Hybrid search combining vector retrieval with graph-based backlink ranking
Exposes 30+ MCP tools for integration with Claude and Cursor

pyrate-llama/hermes-ui.summary: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface that serves as a centralized command center for the self-hosted Hermes Agent. It functions as a single-file React 18 application served via a lightweight Python proxy to provide real-time interaction with the agent's core services. The interface enables users to manage chat sessions, monitor live logs, browse local files, and configure MCP tools or cron jobs through a unified dashboard. It also features a dedicated artifact panel for rendering HTML, SVG, and PDF outputs generated by the agent during tasks.
highlights: Single-file React 18 architecture with real-time SSE streaming support
Integrated file browser and memory inspector for local agent data
Multi-tab terminal for Shell, Hermes, and Claude Code workflows

kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc.summary: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.
highlights: Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

AaronWong1999/hermesclaw.summary: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It functions as a centralized iLink poller and proxy server, routing messages to each agent's gateway to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple simultaneous logins. Users can switch between agents or activate multiple brains at once using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /three. The tool also enables voice-based coding via an OpenCode ACP bridge, supporting free models like MiniMax M2.5.
highlights: Enables three AI agents on one WeChat account simultaneously
Routes messages via local proxy servers to prevent token conflicts
Supports switching agents with simple /commands in WeChat chat

stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm.summary: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during conversation compaction. It replaces standard lossy summarization with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that persists every message in a local SQLite store. This architecture allows the agent to use specialized tools like grep and expand to retrieve specific details from previously compacted history. The system ensures bounded context windows for the model while maintaining an unbounded, searchable memory for the agent.
highlights: Hierarchical DAG summaries with three-level escalation for guaranteed convergence
Dedicated agent tools for searching and expanding compacted conversation history
Immutable-first SQLite storage with profile-scoped databases and session filtering

Codename-11/hermes-relay.summary: Hermes-Relay is a companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent through a native Android app and a desktop CLI. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay to bridge local devices with remote agents, allowing for chat, terminal access, and cross-device tool execution. The Android application provides mobile device control including screen reading and gestures, while the desktop client allows a remote agent to execute local tools like file searches and screenshots. This setup eliminates the need for SSH by providing a unified interface for managing remote agent sessions across multiple surfaces.
highlights: Native Android app for chat, voice, and remote device control
Desktop CLI for running remote Hermes agents as local processes
Bidirectional tool execution allowing remote agents to access local files

clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp.summary: Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within the Telegram ecosystem. It establishes a secure connection to a local Hermes installation via a web server, providing a mobile-first dashboard for chat, system monitoring, and task scheduling. The application features a terminal-style UI with support for streaming responses, file attachments, and background agent spawning. Security is handled through dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure only authorized Telegram users can access the agent gateway.
highlights: Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
Real-time system monitoring for CPU, memory, and disk usage
Integrated cron job management to create and trigger scheduled tasks

briancaffey/hermes-otel.summary: hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables comprehensive observability for LLM workflows. It automatically captures and exports model invocations, tool calls, and API requests as OTel spans to any OTLP-compatible backend. The plugin supports a wide range of observability platforms including Phoenix, Langfuse, and SigNoz, allowing developers to track traces, metrics, and logs. It features a multi-backend configuration system and a tiered testing suite to ensure reliable telemetry across different environments.
highlights: Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
Supports Phoenix, Langfuse, LangSmith, SigNoz, and Jaeger backends
Enables parallel telemetry export to multiple backends via YAML configuration

EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui.summary: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that provides a graphical interface for AI interaction and system administration. It centralizes multi-platform channel configuration, session management, and usage analytics into a single responsive web environment. Users can monitor token costs, schedule cron jobs, and manage files across local or remote backends like Docker and SSH. The interface also includes specialized tools for group chat orchestration, model provider management, and real-time log monitoring.
highlights: Unified configuration for eight platforms including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp
Real-time AI chat with session persistence and tool call expansion
